title: PCP Message
description: The transport-neutral PCP envelope. Carries SEMANTIC fields only (type, version, correlation) and exactly one Event. It carries NO transport, trust, identity, routing, signing, delivery, or retry semantics; those belong to the transport layer (e.g. AgentixMesh). A PcpMessage is independently readable and validatable regardless of how it is transported.

pcp_version.description: Protocol version, major.minor. Consumers MUST parse this as two integers, never as a float (so 1.10 > 1.9).

message_id.description: Stable identity for this PCP message, for AUDIT/CORRELATION only. Uniqueness is producer-asserted and is NOT verifiable by PCP. This is NOT a transport/delivery/idempotency id; deduplication and delivery guarantees belong to the transport layer.

message_type.description: A denormalized convenience copy of '<body.entity_type>.<body.verb>' for human/operator readability. It is NOT protocol authority: the normative source is always the body. Validators MUST check that it equals '<body.entity_type>.<body.verb>'; on conflict the body wins. Transports MUST NOT route, authorize, or trust on this field.

issued_at.description: When this PCP message was composed (not when it was delivered).

correlation_id.description: Optional SEMANTIC link to a prior PcpMessage.message_id (project-meaning correlation only, never a transport thread id). PCP gives no guarantee that the referenced message exists, was authentic, or came from the same source. Consumers MUST NOT make security-relevant decisions on it alone.
